title: entada reticulata
intro: Entada reticulata, also known as the "Indian bean" or "river tamarind", is a climbing legume native to tropical Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Entada reticulata include its ability to treat fever, diarrhea, and various skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol and blood pressure. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves, seeds, and roots are typically used to make infusions, decoctions, and tonics, often in combination with other medicinal plants.
